perturbation.original_text: Upon the effective date of this agreement COMWARE shall have the exclusive right to purchase, at the DISCOUNTS described below, and resell Products to potential customers during the Initial Distribution Period, based on the following terms and conditions: (a) COMWARE must purchase no less than $22,710.00 worth of Products, in any combination, by April 15, 2000. This initial order shall be evidenced by valid purchase order from COMWARE to be received by ITS no later than 5:00PM April 1, 2000, with payment to be received by ITS no later than April 15, 2000. In the event either of these dates are not met, this Agreement will automatically and immediately terminate and neither of the parties hereto will have any further obligations, one to the other.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the effective date of this agreement COMWARE shall have the exclusive right to purchase, at the DISCOUNTS described below, and resell Products to potential customers during the Initial Distribution Period, based on the following terms and conditions: (a) COMWARE must purchase no less than $22,710.00 worth of Products, in any combination, by April 15, 2000. This initial order shall be evidenced by valid purchase order from COMWARE to be received by ITS no later than 5:00PM April 1, 2000, with payment to be received by ITS no later than April 15, 2000. However, in the event these dates are not met, ITS has the option to extend the deadline by 15 days and grant COMWARE an extension period.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the consequences of missing the initial order deadlines. Originally, the agreement automatically and immediately terminates. The modified text allows ITS to optionally extend the deadline. This contradicts the original automatic termination clause, creating uncertainty about the enforceability of the termination clause. This contradicts the automatic termination, stated in section 5, in case of not meeting the minimum purchase requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MINIMUM ORDERS TO MAINTAIN AGREEMENT: COMWARE must purchase order a minimum of $4,200 worth of Products per month during the Renewal Period TO maintain the terms and conditions of this Agreement. In the event COMWARE does not meet these minimum purchase requirements, this Agreement will automatically and immediately terminate and neither of the parties hereto will have any further obligations, one to the other.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement will automatically and immediately terminate if the minimum purchase requirements are not met. The changed text introduces the option for ITS to extend the deadline, directly contradicting the automatic termination clause. The contradicted text also states that the agreement will automatically and immediately terminate if the minimum purchase requirements are not met during the Renewal Period. The introduction of an extension option in the changed text creates a clear contradiction regarding the enforceability of the termination clause, making it unclear whether the agreement will automatically terminate or if ITS has the discretion to extend the deadline. This falls under the definition of inconsistencies, as 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.'

perturbation.original_text: ITS will deliver working systems no later than 30 days after a hard copy purchase order is received from COMWARE.
perturbation.changed_text: ITS will deliver working systems no later than 30 days, or up to 60 days, after a hard copy purchase order is received from COMWARE.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to Section 7 introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that ITS will deliver systems within 30 days, but also allowing up to 60 days for delivery. This creates uncertainty and contradicts the original commitment to a 30-day delivery timeframe. This contradicts with the statement on section 3, on delivery schedule approximating 15 days.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Future Purchase Orders and delivery will approximate the 15 day delivery schedule (but not the dates), although payment will be made by COMWARE to ITS no later than thirty (30) days after receipt of invoice from ITS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a clear contradiction. The original text specified delivery within 30 days. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating delivery can be within 30 days or up to 60 days, directly conflicting with the 'Future Purchase Orders and delivery will approximate the 15 day delivery schedule' in the contradicted text. The modification allows for a delivery timeframe that is potentially four times longer than the approximated schedule, creating a direct inconsistency and thus, an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: TERMINATION: except as specifically described elsewhere in this agreement, either ITS or COMWARE shall have the right to terminate this Agreement with 30 days written notice from the other party, for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: TERMINATION: except as specifically described elsewhere in this agreement, either ITS or COMWARE shall have the right to terminate this Agreement with 30 days written notice from the other party, for any reason whatsoever. However, COMWARE must fulfill all outstanding purchase orders prior to the termination taking effect.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a condition for COMWARE's termination rights that is not applied to ITS, creating a contradiction in termination conditions. Section 10 previously allowed either party to terminate with 30 days' notice for any reason. Now, COMWARE is additionally burdened with fulfilling all outstanding purchase orders, which is not mentioned anywhere else in the agreement. This is in contradiction with the automatic termination conditions stipulated in sections 3 and 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event either of these dates are not met, this Agreement will automatically and immediately terminate and neither of the parties hereto will have any further obligations, one to the other.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed either party to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice for any reason. The changed text adds a condition for COMWARE that requires them to fulfill all outstanding purchase orders before termination, creating an inconsistency. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' section, which states that the agreement will automatically terminate if certain dates are not met, without any stipulation about fulfilling outstanding purchase orders. Therefore, the change introduces a conditional termination for COMWARE, which conflicts with the unconditional and automatic termination clause.
